failure_modes.text: Ego depletion makes good judgment harder once mental effort and attention are exhausted.
failure_modes.mitigation: Design the environment, not the willpower: when self-control is depleted, use defaults, checklists, and pre-commitments to protect good decisions from fatigue.

failure_modes.text: Reactive firefighting can crowd out important work even when priorities are already known.
failure_modes.mitigation: Protect priority time structurally: block calendar time for important-not-urgent work and route reactive requests through a triage filter instead of relying on in-the-moment discipline.

failure_modes.text: Self-control misfires when it becomes a character judgment instead of a design problem.
failure_modes.mitigation: Reframe self-control failures as system failures: when people consistently fail to follow through, redesign the process, incentives, or workload rather than blaming character.

premortem_questions.text: Am I rested enough to make this choice deliberately instead of impulsively?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Am I physically and mentally rested enough

premortem_questions.text: Is this task important, or am I just putting out a fire?
What minimum viable standard is good enough to act without perfectionism?

heuristics.text: Pause and ask what is happening before acting on the first impulse.
Use the Eisenhower matrix to protect important work from reactive drift.
Shape behavior through routines and prompts instead of relying on raw willpower alone.
